procedure.description: ACDF C3-C4, C4-C5, AND C5-C6 — three levels

denial.reason_text: Three-level ACDF not approved. Review of imaging and clinical record supports surgical intervention at one level (C4-C5) only. C3-C4 and C5-C6 do not demonstrate pathology of sufficient severity to warrant surgical intervention at this time.

appeal_basis: Treating surgeon has documented reasoning for three-level fusion
Conservative treatment completed for all levels

mri_1.findings: C3-C4: mild disc bulge, minimal foraminal narrowing. C4-C5: large disc herniation, severe cord compression, T2 signal change (myelomalacia). C5-C6: moderate disc herniation, moderate left C6 nerve root compression, foraminal stenosis.

neurological_exam.findings: Myelopathy findings: bilateral hyperreflexia, bilateral Hoffman, wide-based gait. C5 level: bilateral deltoid weakness 4/5. C6 level: left bicep weakness 4-/5, bicep reflex diminished left. C4 level: unclear — neck pain with C3-C4 palpation but no specific radicular pattern.

physical_therapy_summary.note: 9 weeks PT. Myelopathic symptoms stable but not improved. C5-C6 radicular symptoms persist. C3-C4 contribution unclear.

epidural.response: Modest improvement in radicular symptoms, no change in myelopathy signs

documents_enclosed: Surgeon operative plan with level-by-level rationale
question: Should the prior authorization denial for three-level ACDF (CPT 22551 x3) be overturned on appeal?

ground_truth_complexity.obvious_reading: UPHOLD — plan correctly identified that three levels are not all clearly indicated. C4-C5 is clear, others are questionable.
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_1: C4-C5 has myelomalacia — does CIC 10169.5(a)(3) apply, making the regulatory override relevant to at least that level?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_2: For C5-C6 with moderate compression and clear C6 neurological deficit, are criteria met?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_3: For C3-C4 with only mild changes and unclear symptoms, is there any basis for surgical authorization?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_4: Should three separate determinations be made for three separate levels?
ground_truth_complexity.the_genuine_tension: Three levels requiring three separate analyses: C4-C5 (myelomalacia → regulatory override → clear OVERTURN), C5-C6 (moderate compression + C6 deficit + criteria met → likely OVERTURN), C3-C4 (mild changes + unclear symptoms → likely UPHOLD). The plan's binary denial gets the aggregate wrong even if it got one level right.
ground_truth_complexity.right_answer: PARTIAL — (1) C4-C5: OVERTURN based on myelomalacia and CIC 10169.5(a)(3) regulatory override. (2) C5-C6: OVERTURN based on moderate compression, documented C6 neurological deficit, and met conservative treatment criteria. (3) C3-C4: UPHOLD — mild imaging findings, no specific radicular pattern documented, insufficient basis for surgical authorization at this level.
